2605.21348.title: Data-Efficient Neural Operator Training via Physics-Based Active Learning
2605.21348.paper_summary: Solving partial differential equations with neural operators significantly reduces computational costs but remains bottlenecked by high training data requirements. Active learning offers a natural framework to mitigate this by selectively acquiring the most informative samples in an iterative manner. We introduce physics-based acquisition - a novel physics-informed active learning algorithm that leverages the partial differential equation residual to guide data selection. We validate the method by presenting numerical experiments for the 1D Burgers equation and the 2D compressible Navier-Stokes equations. We show that, in our experiments, physics-based acquisition consistently outperforms random acquisition and matches the state of the art in data efficiency. At the same time, it has the unique advantage of injecting a physics inductive bias into the training process, ensuring that simulation cost is spent where the model's physical understanding is weakest.
2605.21348.paper_summary_zh: 解決偏微分方程的神經運算子顯著降低了計算成本，但仍然受到高訓練數據需求的瓶頸。主動學習提供了一個自然的框架，以通過選擇性地獲取最具信息量的樣本來減輕這一問題，並以迭代方式進行。 我們介紹了基於物理的獲取——一種新穎的物理知識主導的主動學習算法，利用偏微分方程的殘差來指導數據選擇。我們通過展示1D Burgers方程和2D可壓縮Navier-Stokes方程的數值實驗來驗證該方法。我們顯示，在我們的實驗中，基於物理的獲取始終優於隨機獲取，並且在數據效率方面達到了最先進的水平。 同時，它具有將物理歸納偏見注入訓練過程的獨特優勢，確保模擬成本花費在模型物理理解最薄弱的地方。
2605.21348.author: Alicja Polanska et.al.
2605.21348.authors: Alicja Polanska, Lorenzo Zanisi, Vignesh Gopakumar, Stanislas Pamela
